sglang.title: [FEAT] JSON constrained support
sglang.author: havetc
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
A lot of llm API (Together AI, fireworks, Anyscale...) and other engines (vllm...) support constrained generation with a JSON schema. As outlines is already a dependency of sglang, it is straightforward to extend its usage to directly support json schema in the API.

sglang.author: caiyueliang
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
When I was utilizing the interfaces related to /v1/batches, I discovered that sglang does not currently support the 'Cancel batch' feature as described in the OpenAI documentation here. As a result, I am making an effort to implement this feature.

vllm.title: [Performance][BlockManagerV2] Mark prefix cache block as computed after schedule
vllm.author: comaniac
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Closes #7619
With the investigation in #7619, the root cause of block manager v2 low throughput with prefix caching is that block manager v2 doesn't mark prefix cache hit blocks as computed right after scheduling a batch. Specifically, the life cycle of a prefix cache block is as follows:

The block

vllm.title: [misc] fix custom allreduce p2p cache file generation
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
previously, we directly use the output of the test process to unpickle.
however, that process might have some warning messages, e.g. in #7846
we should add markers to mark the start and end of the data.

vllm.title: [Bugfix] neuron: enable tensor parallelism
vllm.author: omrishiv
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
FILL IN THE PR DESCRIPTION HERE
FIX #6269
Following #7175, this enables tensor parallelism on neuron with vLLM > 0.5.0. The block-size choices are also reverted back to the previous values and vLLM will take the max-model-len as the block-size on neuron devices.

vllm.title: [Misc] Update compressed tensors lifecycle to remove `prefix` from `create_weights`
vllm.author: dsikka
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary

Update compressed tensors to match the lifecycle of other quant methods for when unqunatized layers are initialized
Update when the scheme is selected to eliminate the need for prefix in create_weights
Tested against a non-uniform model from llm-compressor

vllm.title: [Core] Asynchronous Output Processor
vllm.author: megha95
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
This PR is an implementation for RFC #6913 .
Following changes were made:

Scheduler states' updates is decoupled from output processor. Earlier both _process_model_outputs and scheduler.schedule() were updating deque and free-ing memory of finished sequences.
Adds _advance_to_next_step in LLMEngine

vllm.title: [Tests] Disable retries and use context manager for openai client
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The openai python client by default retries failed requests up to two times. In our tests I think we should disable this to avoid hiding issues.
Doing this actually caused some failures (at least with another PR I'm working on), which seem to be related to how a single client fixture is shared betwe

vllm.title: [core][torch.compile] not compile for profiling
vllm.author: youkaichao
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
we only profile once to determine the space for kv-cache, and don't run profile anymore.
compiling this run will only add guards and code cache for useless code.
removing this compilation can reduce the overhead of dynamp.
